rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign melasma with acute health-anxiety — routine reassurance/self-care with a skin-check safety-net; not urgent. Manage the panic supportively.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A single pigmented spot becoming asymmetric, irregular, raised, changing, or bleeding
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: See a GP for a skin check (within weeks; sooner if rapidly changing)
escalation_edges.rationale: Would need dermoscopy/biopsy to exclude melanoma.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If any single spot becomes uneven, raised, changing colour or bleeds, see your GP for a skin check.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Systemic features: marked weakness, dizziness, vomiting, or darkening of palm creases/gums
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become weak, dizzy or start vomiting, or your palm creases/gums darken, seek medical review promptly.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Partner can support anxiety de-escalation; provide clear written reassurance and follow-up plan.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is benign melasma, not skin cancer. Use SPF 50+ daily and a hat, avoid scrubbing your skin, and see your GP in a few weeks to review your pill and optional treatments. Keep up routine skin checks.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Routine GP in 2-4 weeks; sooner if a single spot changes or you feel systemically unwell
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed; routine GP review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document benign melasma (melanoma/Addison's screened negative), sun protection advised, OCP review, and anxiety support.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without screening for a single atypical lesion or systemic features.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A coincidental melanoma or early Addison's is missed amid blanket reassurance.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No sun-protection advice or follow-up, and no anxiety support.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending benign symmetrical melasma to ED for suspected 'skin cancer'.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot dermoscope/palpate remotely, but the symmetrical smooth pattern is characteristic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure after screening for a focal atypical lesion and systemic features, advise sun protection, and keep a skin-check safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Blanket reassurance without screening, or over-escalation.
